content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA of length NNN. In one operation, Chef can choose any element AiA_iAi​ and split it into two **positive** integers XXX and YYY such that X+Y=AiX+Y = A_iX+Y=Ai​.

Note that the length of array increases by 111 after every operation.

Determine the **minimum** numbers of operations required by Chef to make parity of all the elements **same**.

It is guaranteed that parity of all the elements can be made equal after applying the above operation zero or more times.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the length of array AAA.
Next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,A3,…,ANA_1, A_2, A_3, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,A3​,…,AN​ - denoting the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output the **minimum** number of operations required to make parity of all elements same.
constraints: Sum of NNN does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: All the elements have the same parity since all are odd. Thus, we need zero operations.
Test case 222: In one operation, we can split the element A2=2A_2 = 2A2​=2 to X=1X = 1X=1 and Y=1Y = 1Y=1 such that X+Y=2X+Y = 2X+Y=2. Thus, the array now becomes A=[1,1,1,3]A = [1, 1, 1, 3]A=[1,1,1,3]. All the elements have the same parity since all are odd. Thus, we need only one operation.
